name: kubectl_get
description: Get or list Kubernetes resources by resource type, name, and optionally namespace

resourceType.description: Type of resource to get (e.g., pods, deployments, services, configmaps, events, etc.)

name.description: Name of the resource (optional - if not provided, lists all resources of the specified type)

allNamespaces.description: If true, list resources across all namespaces

name: kubectl_describe
description: Describe Kubernetes resources by resource type, name, and optionally namespace

resourceType.description: Type of resource to describe (e.g., pods, deployments, services, etc.)

name.description: Name of the resource to describe

allNamespaces.description: If true, describe resources across all namespaces

name: kubectl_logs
description: Get logs from Kubernetes resources like pods, deployments, or jobs

resourceType.description: Type of resource to get logs from

container.description: Container name if the pod has multiple containers

tail.description: Number of lines to show from end of logs

previous.description: Include logs from previously terminated containers

follow.description: Follow logs output (not recommended, may cause timeouts)

name: kubectl_context
description: Manage Kubernetes contexts - list, get, or set the current context

operation.description: Operation to perform: list contexts, get current context, or set current context

name.description: Name of the context to set as current (required for set operation)

verbose.description: Include detailed information about the context

name: kubectl_apply
description: Apply a Kubernetes YAML manifest from a string or file

filePath.description: Path to a YAML file to apply

name: kubectl_delete
description: Delete Kubernetes resources by resource type, name, labels, or from a manifest file

resourceType.description: Type of resource to delete (e.g., pods, deployments, services, etc.)

name.description: Name of the resource to delete

manifest.description: YAML manifest defining resources to delete (optional)

filePath.description: Path to a YAML file to delete resources from (optional)

allNamespaces.description: If true, delete resources across all namespaces

name: kubectl_create
description: Create Kubernetes resources using various methods (from file or using subcommands)

dryRun.description: If true, validate resource schema against server schema

manifest.description: YAML manifest to create resources from

filePath.description: Path to a YAML file to create resources from

name.description: Name of the resource to create

image.description: Image to use for the containers in the deployment

replicas.description: Number of replicas to create for the deployment

name: kubectl_patch
description: Update field(s) of a resource using strategic merge patch, JSON merge patch, or JSON patch

name.description: Name of the resource to patch

patch.description: Patch data as a JSON object

name: kubectl_scale
description: Scale a Kubernetes deployment

name.description: Name of the deployment to scale

replicas.description: Number of replicas to scale to

name: kubectl_rollout
description: Manage the rollout of a resource (e.g., deployment, daemonset, statefulset)

resourceType.description: Type of resource to manage rollout for

revision.description: Revision to rollback to (for undo subcommand)

watch.description: Watch the rollout status in real-time until completion

name: kubectl_generic
description: Execute any kubectl command with the provided arguments and flags

command.description: The kubectl command to execute (e.g. patch, rollout, top)

name: ping
description: Verify that the counterpart is still responsive and the connection is alive.

name: port_forward
description: Forward a local port to a port on a Kubernetes resource

name: stop_port_forward
description: Stop a port-forward process

resourceName.description: Name of the resource whose port-forward to stop

name: node_management
description: Manage Kubernetes nodes with cordon, drain, and uncordon operations

nodeName.description: Name of the node to operate on (required for cordon, drain, uncordon)

force.description: Force the operation even if there are errors

gracePeriod.description: Period of time in seconds given to each pod to terminate

name: install_helm_chart
description: Install a Helm chart with support for both standard and template-based installation

chart.description: Chart name (e.g., 'nginx') or path to chart directory

namespace.description: Namespace to install the chart in

repoUrl.description: Helm repository URL (optional if using local chart path)

values.description: Custom values to override chart defaults

valuesFile.description: Path to values file (alternative to values object)

createNamespace.description: Create namespace if it doesn't exist

name: upgrade_helm_chart
description: Upgrade an existing Helm chart release

values.description: Custom values to override chart defaults

name: uninstall_helm_chart
description: Uninstall a Helm chart release

name.description: Name of the Helm release to uninstall

name: exec_in_pod
description: Execute a command in a Kubernetes pod or container and return the output. Command must be an array of strings where the first element is the executable and remaining elements are arguments. This executes directly without shell interpretation for security.

podName.description: Name of the pod to execute the command in

command.description: Command to execute as an array of strings (e.g. ["ls", "-la", "/app"]). First element is the executable, remaining are arguments. Shell operators like pipes, redirects, or command chaining are not supported - use explicit array format for security.

container.description: Container name (required when pod has multiple containers)

timeout.description: Timeout for command - 60000 milliseconds if not specified

name: explain_resource
description: Get documentation for a Kubernetes resource or field

resource.description: Resource name or field path (e.g. 'pods' or 'pods.spec.containers')

recursive.description: Print the fields of fields recursively

name: list_api_resources
description: List the API resources available in the cluster

namespaced.description: If true, only show namespaced resources

context.description: Kubeconfig Context to use for the command (optional - defaults to null)

verbs.description: List of verbs to filter by

name: cleanup
description: Cleanup all managed resources
